title: Past perfect
explanation:
Past perfect

had + past participle

We use the past perfect to show that one action happened before another past action.

  • When I arrived, they had already left.
  • She was nervous because she had never flown before.

questions.text: When we got to the station, the train [*had left|left|has left].
questions.feedback: The train left before we got there.

questions.text: She was upset because she [*had lost|lost|has lost] her keys.
questions.feedback: The loss happened before the feeling.

questions.text: I [*had never seen|never saw|have never seen] snow before I moved to Norway.
By the time he called, I [*had finished|finished|have finished] dinner.
questions.feedback: By the time signals earlier past action.

questions.text: They [*had already booked|already booked|have already booked] the tickets when I asked.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: After she ____ the report, she went home. | *had sent | sent | has sent
We realised we ____ there before. | *had been | were | have been
He ____ dinner before the guests arrived. | *had prepared | prepared | has prepared
I was tired because I ____ very little the night before. | *had slept | slept | have slept
The film started after everyone [*had sat down|sat down|has sat down].
questions.feedback: The sitting happened before the start.

questions.text: She did not want dessert because she [*had eaten|ate|has eaten] too much.
questions.feedback: Earlier action caused the later decision.

questions.text: I knew the city because I [*had visited|visited|have visited] it before.
By 2015, they [*had moved|moved|have moved] three times.
questions.feedback: By + past year often takes past perfect.

questions.text: The teacher was angry because no one [*had done|did|has done] the homework.
questions.feedback: Homework should have been done earlier.
